name: aerodynamic-settle
_notes: Drag the page to roughly π/2 then release with near-zero velocity. The aerodynamic settle (?settle=aero, PRD #9) should: (1) emit a settle-start{mode:'aero'} event when the pointer is released, (2) emit a matching settle-end{mode:'aero', target:1} after the coupled φ/b oscillator converges — proving the new integrator drove the page all the way home rather than the legacy 1-DOF energy-based path, and (3) emit the settle-end within ~3 s of release. The withinMsAfterT bound also catches the issue-#29 failure mode where settle reverts to the edge-bend model on release: that produced a sub-100ms snap-back to a frozen edge-bend, never reaching the aero settle-end event.

assertions.description: Releasing the page enters the aerodynamic settle path (?settle=aero), not the legacy 1-DOF energy integrator.

The coupled φ/b oscillator converges to commit (target=1) within ~3 s of release — proves the aero integrator drives the turn to completion rather than snapping back to the edge-bend model (issue #29).
